# Codespace

**Manage Multiple Coding Projects & Automate Tasks on Windows — All in One Place**

---

## What is Codespace?

Codespace is a free Windows desktop app built for developers who are tired of **organizing project files and folders** by hand. Whether you're handling multiple projects simultaneously in **Python, Java, JavaScript, C++, C, HTML** or any other language — Codespace gives you one clean control panel to organize project files and folders and manage them all.

- 📁 **Organize** — Keep every project's file and folder structure in one list
- ⚡ **Automate** — Run scripts in any language automatically with zero manual triggering
- 🖥 **Open** — Launch any project in your favourite IDE with one click
- 🗜 **Export** — Package any project as a `.zip` file instantly

> Codespace is designed for **beginners managing their first few projects** all the way to **intermediate and advanced developers handling multiple coding projects simultaneously**.

---

## Installation

1. Go to [codespaceapp.com/download](https://codespace-desktop.github.io/Codespace/download)
2. Download the **Windows installer** (`.exe`)
3. Run the installer — if Windows SmartScreen appears, click **More info → Run anyway**
4. Launch Codespace from the Start Menu or desktop shortcut
5. No account required. Free to use.

**System Requirements:**

| Requirement | Minimum |
|---|---|
| OS | Windows 10 or Windows 11 (64-bit) |
| RAM | 4 GB |
| Disk Space | 200 MB free |
| IDE Support | Any installed IDE (VS Code, JetBrains, Sublime, etc.) |

---

## Projects

Codespace uses a clean **software project directory structure** for every project you add:

- **Root directory** — the top-level folder containing every file and folder in the project
- **Source directory (`src`)** — only your project's code files; this is where imported projects land

This structure makes it easy to manage multiple coding projects without your **software project files getting scattered** across your machine.

---

### Create a Project

Add any coding project — Python, Java, JavaScript, C++, C, HTML, or any language — to Codespace in seconds.

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace
2. Click **Add**
3. Enter a **Project name**
4. Choose the **Root directory** (the folder containing every file and folder in your project)
5. Choose the **Source directory** (`src`) — your project's code files, and where imported files will be copied
6. Enter the **Command Prompt command** to run the project (include `cd` to navigate to the directory first)
7. Click **OK**

**CLI:**

```bat
codespace create <name> <rootDir> <srcDir> <runCommand>
```

```bat
:: Example — Python project
codespace create MyPythonApp "C:\Projects\MyPythonApp" "C:\Projects\MyPythonApp\src" "cd C:\Projects\MyPythonApp && python main.py"

:: Example — Java project
codespace create MyJavaApp "C:\Projects\MyJavaApp" "C:\Projects\MyJavaApp\src" "cd C:\Projects\MyJavaApp && java -jar app.jar"

:: Example — JavaScript / Node project
codespace create MyNodeApp "C:\Projects\MyNodeApp" "C:\Projects\MyNodeApp\src" "cd C:\Projects\MyNodeApp && node index.js"
```

> ✅ Works with any language — Python, Java, JavaScript, C++, C, HTML, and more.

---

### Import a Project

When **handling multiple projects simultaneously**, you often need to copy files from one project into another. Codespace copies all source files from one project's `src` folder into another project's `src` folder.

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace
2. Click on the **destination project** (the one you want to import files into)
3. The project dashboard opens
4. Click **Import Project**
5. Select the project to import from — all its `src` files are copied across

**CLI:**

```bat
codespace import <destinationProjectName> <importingProjectName>
```

```bat
:: Example
codespace import MyMainApp MyLibrary
```

---

### Run a Project

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace
2. Click on the project
3. Click **Settings → Run**

**CLI:**

```bat
codespace run <projectName>
```

---

### Open in IDE

Open any project in whichever editor you have configured — VS Code, JetBrains (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm), Sublime Text, or any other.

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace
2. Click on the project
3. Click **Open in IDE**
4. Select your editor

> ⚠️ No CLI command available for this feature. Configure your IDEs first — see [Configure an IDE](#configure-an-ide).

---

### Export to ZIP

Package any project's `src` directory as a `.zip` file — great for sharing, backing up, or moving a project.

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace
2. Click on the project
3. Click **Settings → Export to ZIP**

The `.zip` is saved in the same directory as the project's root folder.

**CLI:**

```bat
codespace exportZIP <projectName> <destinationFolder>
```

```bat
:: Example
codespace exportZIP MyPythonApp "C:\Backups"
```

---

### Delete a Project

Removes the project entry from Codespace. **Does not delete files from your disk.**

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace
2. Click on the project
3. Click **Settings → Delete Project**

**CLI:**

```bat
codespace delete <projectName>
```

---

## Automations

Automations let you **automate tasks on your computer** by attaching a command to a trigger — before or after a project runs. You can **automate tasks with Python**, bash, Node.js, PowerShell, or any language that runs natively on Windows. This is the most effective way to reduce repetitive developer busywork when managing multiple coding projects.

---

### Create an Automation

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace
2. Select the **Automations** tab
3. Click **Add**
4. Enter an **Automation name**
5. Enter the name of the **project or automation** this should run before or after
6. Set **After Action** to `true` to run after, or `false` to run before
7. Enter the **Command Prompt command** to execute (include `cd` to navigate to the correct directory)
8. Click **OK**

**CLI:**

```bat
codespace automate <automationName> <command> <afterAction: true|false> <actionName>
```

```bat
:: Example — automate tasks with Python: run a cleanup script after "MyApp" runs
codespace automate Cleanup "cd C:\Scripts && python cleanup.py" true MyApp

:: Example — automate tasks in Windows: run a build script before project starts
codespace automate PreBuild "cd C:\MyApp && npm run build" false MyApp
```

**Run Automation on Codespace Open or Close:**

1. Open Codespace
2. Go to **Settings** tab
3. Assign an automation under **Run on Open** or **Run on Close**

---

### Run an Automation

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace → **Automations** tab
2. Click the automation
3. Click **Run**

**CLI:**

```bat
codespace runAutomation <automationName>
```

---

### Delete an Automation

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace → **Automations** tab
2. Click the automation
3. Click **Delete**

**CLI:**

```bat
codespace deleteAutomation <automationName>
```

---

## IDEs

Register any installed editor so Codespace can open your projects directly in it.

### Configure an IDE

**GUI:**

1. Open Codespace → **IDEs** tab
2. Click **Configure IDE**
3. Browse to the IDE's **`.exe` file path**
4. Enter a **name** for the IDE
5. If the IDE uses a custom open command (not `<exe> <projectFolder>`), enter the full command — replace the exe path with `<Path>` and the project folder with `<Project>` *(case-sensitive)*
6. Click **OK**

> ⚠️ No CLI command available for configuring IDEs.

**Supported editors include** (but are not limited to):
- Visual Studio Code
- JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, CLion)
- Sublime Text
- Notepad++
- Any editor with a CLI open command

---

## CLI Reference

### Setting up the CLI

The Codespace CLI lets you manage multiple coding projects — in any language — directly from your terminal, from any directory on your machine.

**Setup (one time only):**

1. Copy the full path of your Codespace installation directory
2. Add it to your **System Environment Variable `Path`**
3. Open a new terminal — you can now run `codespace` from anywhere

> ✅ Every successfully executed command outputs `success.` at the end when there are no internal errors.

---

### All Commands

| Command | Description | Syntax |
|---|---|---|
| `codespace create` | Create and register a new project | `<name> <rootDir> <srcDir> <runCmd>` |
| `codespace delete` | Remove a project from Codespace | `<name>` |
| `codespace run` | Run a project using its configured command | `<name>` |
| `codespace import` | Copy source files from one project into another | `<destination> <source>` |
| `codespace exportZIP` | Export a project's `src` as a `.zip` | `<name> <destinationFolder>` |
| `codespace get` | Retrieve a project's metadata JSON | `<name>` |
| `codespace automate` | Create an automation tied to a project or action | `<name> <cmd> <afterAction> <actionName>` |
| `codespace runAutomation` | Manually trigger an automation | `<automationName>` |
| `codespace deleteAutomation` | Delete an automation | `<automationName>` |

**Retrieve project metadata:**

```bat
codespace get <projectName>
```

This outputs a JSON metadata object ready to import into another Codespace instance.

---

## Supported Languages

Codespace works with **any language that runs on Windows**. Here are some common examples:

| Language | Example run command |
|---|---|
| Python | `cd C:\MyApp && python main.py` |
| Java | `cd C:\MyApp && java -jar app.jar` |
| JavaScript / Node | `cd C:\MyApp && node index.js` |
| C / C++ | `cd C:\MyApp && .\build\app.exe` |
| HTML (live server) | `cd C:\MyApp && npx live-server` |
| Batch / PowerShell | `cd C:\MyApp && .\start.bat` |
